data.description:
S. with a 34% tariff, sending shockwaves through Wall Street and crypto. Bitcoin, which had recently shown signs of recovery, climbing above $84K, took a hit as Nasdaq futures plunged further

data.title: VIX Reaches Highest Level Since 2020, Indicating Market Volatility
data.description: According to BlockBeats, data from TradingView reveals that the VIX, a key measure of volatility for the S&P 500 and often referred to as Wall Street's 'fear index,' has surged to its highest level since October 2020. The index briefly exceeded 45 and is currently reported at 43.7, signaling increased market uncertainty.

data.title: Analysis: Long-term holders have increased their holdings by 400,000 Bitcoins since February, and market confidence remains strong
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Since the beginning of April, the market confidence of both short-term and long-term holders of Bitcoin (BTC) has increased abnormally. Glassnode data shows that short-term holders (users who have held Bitcoin for less than 155 days) have increased the number of Bitcoins held since the beginning of April by about 15,000, and the total holdings are currently slightly more than 3.7 million.
Long-term holders (users who have held for at least 155 days) have increased their Bitcoin reserves by 400,000 since February, and have purchased a small amount of Bitcoin this month, with a total holding of more than 13.5 million, indicating that even in the case of recent price stagnation, the confidence of long-term holders is still increasing. (CoinDesk)

data.title: Trader Says Ethereum Competitor at a ‘Generational’ Entry Point, Outlines Path Forward for Bitcoin and FLOKI
data.description:
A closely followed crypto strategist believes that one Ethereum (ETH) competitor may have reached a local bottom amid a broad market sell-off. Pseudonymous analyst Inmortal tells his 231,100 followers on the social media platform X that Solana (SOL) may be presenting a rare buying opportunity after hitting the 24-hour low of $112.
source: https://dailyhodl.com/2025/04/04/trader-says-ethereum-competitor-at-a-generational-entry-point-outlines-path-forward-for-bitcoin-and-floki/

data.title: Woman Accidentally Throws Away Husband’s Flash Drive with $3.8 Million in Bitcoin
data.description:
A British woman is grappling with the devastating loss of a fortune after mistakenly discarding her husband's flash drive, which contained $3. 8M Bitcoin.
source: https://zycrypto.com/woman-accidentally-throws-away-husbands-flash-drive-with-3-8-million-in-bitcoin/

data.title: Bitcoin and Gold Set to Shine Amid Tariff Turmoil, Says Arthur Hayes
data.description:
TL;DR Tariff Turmoil Sparks Market Volatility: U. S. tariffs, including a 10% blanket rate and higher levies on China, the EU, and Japan, have triggered historic market drops, notably a 1,060-point fall in the Nasdaq 100. Bitcoin’s Bullish Outlook: Arthur Hayes predicts Bitcoin will shine as a hedge against inflation—bolstered by a weakening U. S. dollar and. . . 
source: https://crypto-economy.com/bitcoin-and-gold-set-to-shine-amid-tariff-turmoil-says-arthur-hayes/

data.title: Greek.live: The community is generally bearish, and Bitcoin's bearish target extends to $40,000
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Greek.live macro researcher Adam released an English community briefing, which pointed out that the community generally holds a bearish sentiment, and most traders expect prices to fall in the coming weeks, mainly affected by macro concerns centered on Trump's tariffs. Bitcoin's key price monitoring levels include $80,000 as an immediate support level, and the bearish target extends to $40,000. Only a few isolated bullish voices believe that it is possible to reach $100,000 in a few months. Traders believe that tariffs have destroyed market trust, and even if tariffs are reduced before the April 9 deadline, the damage to trade relations has already been done.

data.title: New Trade Tariffs Could Impact Global Bitcoin Mining Ecosystem
data.description: According to Odaily, U.S. President Donald Trump's announcement of new trade tariffs may exert additional pressure on the global Bitcoin mining ecosystem. Kristian Csepcsar, Chief Marketing Officer of BTC mining technology provider Braiins, noted that high-end chips used in cryptocurrency mining are predominantly manufactured in Asian countries affected by these tariffs. He highlighted that it could take the United States a decade to catch up in advanced chip manufacturing, potentially leading to short-term losses for domestic mining companies. Meanwhile, countries like Russia and Kazakhstan are intensifying their mining efforts and may surpass the United States in hash rate dominance.

data.title: Opinion: Trump's tariffs may cause short-term losses for US Bitcoin mining companies
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: The new trade tariffs announced by Trump may bring additional pressure to the global Bitcoin mining ecosystem. Kristian Csepcsar, chief marketing officer of BTC mining technology provider Braiins, said that high-end chips used in crypto mining are currently produced in large quantities in Asian countries, which have been hit by tariffs respectively, and it will take the United States a decade to catch up with cutting-edge chip manufacturing, so domestic mining companies in the United States may suffer losses in the short term. On the other hand, some countries, including Russia and Kazakhstan, have been stepping up their mining efforts and may surpass the United States in hash rate dominance. (Cointelegraph)
